cityDNA.coreFantasy: A Greek / Hellenic city where Shrine / Sacred Place, Entertainment / Theater, Public Works Authority, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Slums / Informal Settlement, and Hellenic Marble Agon of the Sun Laurel turn coastal edge, seawall edge, harbor basin, coast and seashell, coral stone, pale marble stone, canvas, silk into a distinct civic machine.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Thalorion should feel like an open-air civic theater where shrine, stage, public works, ancestry, and poverty are all visible under coastal light.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Thalorion organizes public life around oath-bound civic performance: its districts convert Shrine / Sacred Place, Entertainment / Theater, Public Works Authority, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Slums / Informal Settlement into a readable system of authority, labor, memory, and movement.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Thalorion should be recognized by Seashell, Coral Stone, Pale Marble Stone, Canvas, Silk, Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, Coast, Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Tide Sorcery, Celestial Projection, Road Warding, and the named anchors Hellenic Marble Agon of the Sun Laurel, Roadside Shrine Beacon, and Gold Seals Tide-Seal Citadel.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: The city belongs to Greek / Hellenic; its civic habits should grow from the sidecar-backed district spine rather than generic fantasy urbanism.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Hellenic Marble Agon of the Sun Laurel, Roadside Shrine Beacon, Gold Seals Tide-Seal Citadel, and the five section landmark families symbolize oath-bound civic performance while warning against beautiful public order leaving informal lives exposed.

canonLocks: Thalorion is a Greek / Hellenic city, not a generic settlement with cosmetic motifs.
The Wonder is Hellenic Marble Agon of the Sun Laurel.
The Beacon is Roadside Shrine Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Gold Seals Tide-Seal Citadel, located at the District 13 authority center.
The five district sections are Shrine / Sacred Place, Entertainment / Theater, Public Works Authority, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Slums / Informal Settlement.
The board should be treated as twenty-five district slots grouped into five section identities.
Section 1 (Shrine / Sacred Place) preserves these exact anchor names: Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine Beacon, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook.
Section 2 (Entertainment / Theater) preserves these exact anchor names: Entertainment / Theater Civic Hall, Opera Court, Entertainment Theater Lantern Monument, Mask Pavilion Nook.
Section 3 (Public Works Authority) preserves these exact anchor names: Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook.
Section 4 (Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter) preserves these exact anchor names: Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall, Ancestor Vault, Ancestor Masks Monument, Bone Lantern Path Nook.
Section 5 (Slums / Informal Settlement) preserves these exact anchor names: Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook.
provisionalCanon: Individual district-slot names beyond exact sidecar anchors are organizational labels for the 25-seat board, not new landmark canon.
Minor street customs should be derived from the section spine unless future source files provide more exact anchors.
rejectedReads: Thalorion as a generic fantasy city without its named Wonder, Beacon, and Seat of Power.
Replacing exact sidecar anchor names with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, wall, or archive.
Treating district section labels as decorative instead of civic systems that drive scenes and assets.
cautionFlags: Preserve exact named anchors from sidecars.
Use the culture identity respectfully as civic design language rather than costume.
Keep all 25 district slots legible even when five slots share one section identity.
Do not add unsupported individual district landmark names beyond the source-backed anchors.

environment.environmentalLogic: Thalorion's environment should make Shrine / Sacred Place, Entertainment / Theater, Public Works Authority, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Slums / Informal Settlement feel physically necessary. Terrain, light, civic routes, and landmark placement must support movement through all five section identities rather than read as interchangeable scenery.

forms: seashell and polished white limestone materials
skyline: Hellenic Marble Agon of the Sun Laurel is the wonder-scale skyline identifier.
Gold Seals Tide-Seal Citadel marks the central authority mass at District 13.
Roadside Shrine Beacon must remain visible as the public signal landmark.
Section landmarks should form a readable ring of Shrine / Sacred Place, Entertainment / Theater, Public Works Authority, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Slums / Informal Settlement.

civilization.people: Thalorion's people are Greek / Hellenic; they should be written through shared civic habits, public spaces, work rhythms, and the visible demands of the five section identities.
socialClasses: officials and stewards tied to Gold Seals Tide-Seal Citadel
landmark keepers and civic hall administrators
street vendors, porters, runners, and maintenance crews
families living around the section-specific local anchors
civilization.governance: Authority centers on Gold Seals Tide-Seal Citadel and is made visible through section civic halls, named landmarks, permissions, public signals, and the citywide response to Roadside Shrine Beacon.
civilization.religion: Religious or ceremonial life should be shown through source-backed shrines, temples, wonders, oath spaces, ancestor sites, or ritual geometry when those appear in the district spine.
customs: citizens orient themselves by the section identity of a street before they identify an address
public notices and signals are interpreted through the Beacon and civic halls
named landmarks act as meeting points, proof points, and local memory anchors
section-specific work routines shape food, clothing, posture, and street traffic
District 13 authority matters even when scenes occur in outer district slots
bodyLanguage: citizens check landmarks and signals before crossing into another section
officials use the posture and tools of their section role
workers treat section anchors as practical wayfinding points
crowds become more formal near the Seat of Power and more local near minor landmarks
speechPatterns: directions use exact landmark names rather than generic descriptions
official speech references permits, seals, routes, oaths, or signals as appropriate to the city
working speech is concrete, location-heavy, and tied to civic function
locals distinguish between the Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section landmarks without confusion

streets.appearance: Street scenes should make Thalorion immediately identifiable through Seashell, Coral Stone, Pale Marble Stone, Canvas, Silk, Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, Coast, Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Tide Sorcery, Celestial Projection, Road Warding, and the exact section anchors rather than generic fantasy facades.
streets.movement: Movement flows through five section identities and twenty-five district slots. People navigate by Roadside Shrine Beacon, Gold Seals Tide-Seal Citadel, section civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks.
streets.lighting: Lighting should use Lantern Lit, White Limestone Civic Blocks, Civic Blue Glow, shared fires, symbolic civic object, lanterns, with the Beacon and Seat carrying distinct civic emphasis.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should interact with coastal edge, seawall edge, harbor basin, coast and the city's source-backed materials, altering visibility, travel speed, public gathering, and signal readability.
streets.dayCycle: Daytime emphasizes section labor: Shrine / Sacred Place, Entertainment / Theater, Public Works Authority, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Slums / Informal Settlement. Civic halls, shops, and public works create the main public rhythm.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes should preserve the city's identity through landmark silhouettes, beacon light, watch routes, late shops, ritual spaces, and section-specific sounds.

prestigious: workshops or offices near Gold Seals Tide-Seal Citadel
specialist houses serving Hellenic Marble Agon of the Sun Laurel
master artisans tied to section landmarks

housing.wealthy: High-status housing clusters near Gold Seals Tide-Seal Citadel, major terraces, and prestigious landmark approaches; it should display the city materials cleanly and control sightlines toward Wonder or Beacon.
housing.middle: Middle housing occupies upper rooms, side courts, work-family compounds, and mixed-use streets close to civic halls and section services.
housing.poor: Poorer housing should be practical and specific to local labor, with patched materials, shared courtyards, crowded lanes, and strong dependence on nearby section anchors.

wonder.name: Hellenic Marble Agon of the Sun Laurel
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of oath-bound civic performance made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A wonder-scale skyline anchor using Seashell, Coral Stone, Pale Marble Stone, Canvas, Silk, Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, Coast, and the Greek / Hellenic visual language.
wonder.civicRole: Represents the whole city above the five sections: Shrine / Sacred Place, Entertainment / Theater, Public Works Authority, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Slums / Informal Settlement.
storyHooks: access to Hellenic Marble Agon of the Sun Laurel changes public legitimacy
a citywide ritual reveals hidden pressure
the Wonder becomes a navigation or warning reference

beacon.name: Roadside Shrine Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public signal and shared alarm for Thalorion.
beacon.visualRole: A section-scale signal landmark that must read immediately as the city Beacon.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates public response, civic warnings, and Conjunction-state attention.
storyHooks: someone controls the first interpretation of a signal
the Beacon redirects traffic between sections
a warning exposes which citizens trust civic authority

a sealed record changes public interpretation

districtDioramaGuidance: Create twenty-five district dioramas grouped into five recognizable sections: Shrine / Sacred Place, Entertainment / Theater, Public Works Authority, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Slums / Informal Settlement.
Each district should use its districtNumber and sectionNumber, but avoid baked text labels in imagery.
D13 must read as the Seat of Power district centered on Gold Seals Tide-Seal Citadel.
Repeated section anchors may appear across five related slots, but each slot needs its own street function, crowd density, and prop emphasis.
Use exact source-backed anchor names for file naming, prompt references, and canon cross-checking.
S1 must preserve Shrine / Sacred Place with anchors: Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine Beacon, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook.
S2 must preserve Entertainment / Theater with anchors: Entertainment / Theater Civic Hall, Opera Court, Entertainment Theater Lantern Monument, Mask Pavilion Nook.
S3 must preserve Public Works Authority with anchors: Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook.
S4 must preserve Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter with anchors: Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall, Ancestor Vault, Ancestor Masks Monument, Bone Lantern Path Nook.
S5 must preserve Slums / Informal Settlement with anchors: Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Map backgrounds should keep Hellenic Marble Agon of the Sun Laurel as the skyline symbol, Gold Seals Tide-Seal Citadel at the central civic authority, and Roadside Shrine Beacon as the readable public signal.

Arrange the five section identities so Thalorion does not converge with other cities: Shrine / Sacred Place, Entertainment / Theater, Public Works Authority, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Slums / Informal Settlement.

Use terrain cues (Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, Coast), material cues (Seashell, Coral Stone, Pale Marble Stone, Canvas, Silk), and magic cues (Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Tide Sorcery, Celestial Projection, Road Warding) consistently across district clusters.
Show roads, terraces, gates, courts, or paths that make the twenty-five-district tactical layout plausible.
requiredVisualElements: Hellenic Marble Agon of the Sun Laurel
elementsToAvoid: extra top-level schema sections or embedded generation notes
generic fantasy city assets that ignore exact anchor names
unlabeled invented landmarks replacing sidecar names
flattening five sections into one visual district
treating culture identity as costume rather than civic design
overcrowded detail that hides Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power
